title: Inefficient rebuild_routes on single edge deletion
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/routing.c:598-604

When a single edge is deleted (lifetime=0 DAO), the code calls `rebuild_routes()` which iterates ALL edges and rebuilds ALL routes:

```c
if (lifetime == 0) {
    ...
    (void)rebuild_routes(dm);  // Rebuilds ALL routes
    return false;
}
```

For small networks (E <= 32 routes), this is acceptable. But for larger deployments, deleting one edge triggers O(E * H) work where H = LICHEN_RPL_MAX_HOPS.

The code comment at line 507-519 acknowledges this and suggests incremental updates. For now, the full rebuild ensures correctness but could be optimized if performance becomes an issue.

This is documentation/future-optimization, not a correctness bug.
